title: Corona-class frigate

The '''''Corona''-class frigate''' was a combat [[Starship/Legends|starship]] produced by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] for the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. A successor to the successful [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]], the ''Corona''-class was designed as part of the New Republic's drive to [[New Class Modernization Program|modernize and standardize]] its [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|naval forces]], moving away from the eclectic collection of starships that it had inherited from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. The [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] was designed to incorporate the New Republic's philosophy of tying its ships to a home base for support, and not allowing them to operate independently for [[Standard year/Legends|years]] at a time.

Physically resembling its predecessor, ''Corona''-class vessels were intended to operate both independently and in larger [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] formations. The ship could perform a variety of mission profiles, including reconnaissance, close-line fire support and anti-[[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] screening operations. The ''Corona''-class had an array of offensive and defensive weapons, as well as [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] space for thirty-six starfighters. The frigates were deployed to the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Forces]] by [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], seeing service during the [[Daala campaign|assaults]] on the New Republic by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]].

==Characteristics==
Designed by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]], the ''Corona''-class [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] was patterned after the company's successful [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]]. The ''Corona''-class resembled the profile of the Nebulon-B, with a forward section connected to the drive unit by a central spar. The fore section extended down from the main body of the frigate, while the central spar had been thickened from the original Nebulon-B design to provide extra protection. The drive unit housed the main [[Engine/Legends|engines]], with [[Stabilizer/Legends|stabilizing fins]] extending from the section's lower part on either side. ''Corona''-class [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] required a crew of 782 to operate, along with fifty-six [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]]; however, the frigate could be manned by a skeleton crew of 281. The ships also carried a complement of eighty [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]].<ref name="Threat Dossier" />

The ''Corona''-class was 275 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length,<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> twenty-five meters shorter than its predecessor,<ref name="SWSB">''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''</ref> and had less cargo space for supplies and consumables, in line with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] doctrine of having its ships be reliant on home bases rather than be able to operate independently for [[Standard year/Legends|years]] at a time. ''Corona''-class vessels could carry 4,000 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] of cargo, with enough provisions to last one year before resupply. The vessels were armed with ten [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] cannons and ten [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], as well as four [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] and two [[Tractor beam projector/Legends|tractor beam projectors]]. The frigate's [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] provided the same amount of protection<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> as those on the Nebulon-B,<ref name="SWSB" /> but it had a stronger hull, allowing it to absorb more punishment during combat. The ''Corona''-class incorporated a Class One [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]],<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> which was an improvement over the Class Two hyperdrive on the Nebulon-B.<ref name="SWSB" /> Three [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]]—thirty-six [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]—could be deployed aboard the ''Corona''-class,<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> compared to the two squadrons carried on a Nebulon-B frigate.<ref name="SWSB" /> Not all line units carried a full complement, and some were only equipped with short-range fighters.<ref name="Threat Dossier" />

==Role==
The ''Corona''-class frigate was used as a patrol vessel and could fulfill a variety of mission profiles. The vessel could operate independently, performing reconnaissance-in-force, or as part of a larger [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], providing close fire support and anti-starfighter screening for larger vessels. The frigate could also be used as a starfighter assault platform.<ref name="Threat Dossier" />

==History==
As the New Republic began to establish its legitimacy following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the guerrilla conflict that they had fought for years turned into a conventional war. After the New Republic [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberated]] the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] of the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]] saw the need for a regular [[Navy/Legends|navy]] to both prosecute the war against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and to defend their assets. The motley collection of vessels that the New Republic had inherited from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] were no longer sufficient for that purpose. It was decided that new starship classes were to be designed and produced to standardize the New Republic's naval forces.<ref name="Threat Dossier" />

Prior to [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],<ref name="Thrawn Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' establishes the events of''[[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy]]'' to have occurred in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' dates the start of production for the ''Corona''-class to before the events of ''Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy''.</ref> Kuat Drive Yards was commissioned to produce a line frigate, which was modelled after the successful Nebulon-B design that had served both the Rebel Alliance and Galactic Empire during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> Concurrently, the New Republic's [[Defender (ship-design program)|Defender program]] was underway, and the ''Corona''-class was added to the project to allow Kuat Drive Yards to participate, alongside [[Loronar Corporation/Legends|Loronar Corporation's]] [[Belarus-class medium cruiser|''Belarus''-class medium cruiser]].<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Production of the ''Corona''-class was disrupted by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the New Republic<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> in 9 ABY,<ref name="Thrawn Date" /> as well as the [[Operation Shadow Hand|return]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> a [[10 ABY/Legends|year later]].<ref name="Dark Empire Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' establishes that the events of ''[[Dark Empire]]'' to have occurred in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], the year after the events of ''[[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy]]''.</ref> Following these delays, the Defender program was resumed and became part of the [[New Class Modernization Program]].<ref name="EGTW" /> Physically similar to its design predecessor, ''Corona''-class vessels were in service as early<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> as [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]],<ref name="Jedi Academy Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' establishes the events of ''[[Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy]]'' to have occurred in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]].</ref> and several ships saw duty during [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]]'s [[Daala campaign|campaign]] against the New Republic. Orders from the New Republic Defense Force for the ''Corona''-class were modest but steady,<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> resulting in a limited production run of the design.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|144|''Corona''-class frigate}}</ref>

===Notable vessels===
Several ''Corona''-class frigates served the New Republic Defense Forces during the early years of the New Republic. The lead vessel of the class, [[Corona (Corona-class frigate)|''Corona'']], operated in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="Threat Dossier" /> Two vessels, [[Endor (Corona-class frigate)|''Endor'']] and [[Shooting Star (Corona-class frigate)|''Shooting Star'']], collided with each other after a mistimed [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump. Both ships were destroyed, with the [[Death/Legends|loss]] of all hands.<ref name="Before the Storm">''[[Before the Storm]]''</ref> In 11 ABY,<ref name="Jedi Academy Date" /> ''[[Boldheart]]'' encountered the ''[[Teljkon Vagabond]]'', an organic starship used by the [[Qella]] as a genetic repository to ensure the survival of their [[Species/Legends|species]] following an ice age on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Brath Qella]] that threatened their extinction. ''Boldheart'' fired a shot across the ''Vagabond''{{'s}} bow; the ''Vagabond'' responded by disabling the frigate's engines with a single shot before fleeing into hyperspace. The [[Hologram/Legends|holos]] from the encounter provided the [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] with its first images of the ''Vagabond''.<ref name="Before the Storm" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Corona''-class frigate was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1997]]. Appearing in the section dedicated to the events of ''[[The Black Fleet Crisis]]'' trilogy by [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]], its entry established that several vessels mentioned in the novels—''Boldheart'', ''Endor'', and ''Shooting Star''—were of this class.<ref name="Threat Dossier" />
